div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 362px; height: 1px; padding-top: 223px; margin-left: 1370px;

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#2F5B7C, #85abc7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.font: Customer Relationships

div.div: Expect a fast and stable app, available at all times.
Fast response to support queries and bug reports (email, Trello, social media)
Users - Want tips for sticking to new habits and breaking bad habits
Coaches - Want tips for how to find new coaching clients, how to get the best out of the app
- (Copy)writers for tips, social media(costly)
- Translators to translate tips into the languages we support(costly, least return)
- Support staff / development staff who can act as support staff(increasing costs as more users/coaches use the app)
- social media channels and a blog
- Trello and email support contact channels

div.font: What type of relationship does each of our Customer
Segments expect us to establish and maintain with them?
Which ones have we established?
How are they integrated with the rest of our business model?
How costly are they?
Personal assistance, Dedicated Personal Assistance, Self-Service, Automated Services, Communities, Co-creation

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

br.div:
Amazon Web Services - Database hosting
Apple - App Store
Google - Play Store
Trello - Project management, bug and feature tracking
Wordpress - Website
GitHub - Versioned code, technical documentation, mockups and UML diagrams, policies and EULA.

Customers - Feedback and referrals
Coaches - Feedback and referrals
Guest blogs - Feature our app in blog posts

font.font:
Who are our Key Partners?
Who are our Key Suppliers?
Which Key Resources are we acquiring from partners?

div.font: Optimization and economy
Reduction of risk and uncertainty
Acquisition of particular resources and activities

Our Distribution Channels?
Customer Relationships?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 362px; height: 1px; padding-top: 673px; margin-left: 530px;

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#2F5B7C, #85abc7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.font: Key Resources

div.div: App development - programmers, translators, project management tools, beta testers
Marketing / website - copywriters, social media writers, translators, blog post writers
App services - constantly available database hosted by AWS.
App Store listings - Apple, Google
Support channels - Trello, email, social media
Website - ongoing IT support, initial designers
Chat room for development and documentation questions.
Licenses for development and documentation tools (code, screenshots, video)
Wordpress theme (licensed)

div.font: What Key Resources do our Value Propositions require?
Our Distribution Channels? Customer Relationships?
Revenue Streams?
Physical, Intellectual (brand patents, copyrights, data). Human, Financial

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 362px; height: 1px; padding-top: 223px; margin-left: 950px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #2F5B7C;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#2F5B7C, #85abc7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.font: Value Propositions
Improved health (mental and physical) through encouraging healthy habits.
- We make people happier.
- We help users stick to their habits.
- We help them get their job of "wanting to be healthier and happier" done
- We help coaches run a more successful coaching business.
What value do we deliver to the customer?
Which one of our customer’s problems are we helping to solve?
What bundles of products and services are we offering to each Customer Segment?
Which customer needs are we satisfying?
Newness, Performance, Customization, “Getting the Job Done”, Design, Brand/Status, Price, Cost Reduction, Risk Reduction, Accessibility, Convenience/Usability

div.font: Customer Segments

div.div: Users interested in personal development and health improvement (niche)
Coaches helping users improve their habits (niche)

font.i: Doctors / psychologists / physiotherapists? Advising patients to change habits

div.font: For whom are we creating value?
Who are our most important customers?
Mass Market, Niche Market, Segmented, Diversified, Multi-sided Platform

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 362px; height: 1px; padding-top: 673px; margin-left: 1370px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #2F5B7C;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#2F5B7C, #85abc7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.font: Channels
Social media and social sites - Twitter, Reddit, Facebook, Instagram

font.i: Instagram is the most popular, lucrative and efficient for this app

div.font: Guest posts on related personal development and health blogs.
Purchased via Apple App Store and Google Play Store (try to get featured!)
We encourage feedback in app once a month by asking for a review.
When an error screen is shown, we include the Trello link to report a bug.
We participate in reddit AMAs.
Through which Channels do our Customer Segments
want to be reached?
How are we reaching them now?
How are our Channels integrated?
Which ones work best?
Which ones are most cost-efficient?
How are we integrating them with customer routines?
1. Awareness How do we raise awareness about our company’s products?
2. Evaluation How do we help customers evaluate our organization’s Value
Proposition?
3. Purchase How do customers to purchase specific products and services?
4. Delivery How do we deliver a Value Proposition to customers?
5. After sales How do we provide post-purchase customer support?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 992px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1123px; margin-left: 110px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #2F5B7C;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#2F5B7C, #85abc7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.font: Cost Structure
Revenue - paid app.
Value driven - we want to help users develop healthy habits and coaches build a more successful coaching business.
Structure - Freelance writers and developers, distributed team
Risks - Freelancer drop-out, negative reviews.
Costs - variable (phased development, increasing support costs with more users).
What are the most important costs inherent in our business model? Which Key Resources are most expensive? Which Key Activities are most expensive?
Cost Driven (leanest cost structure, low price value proposition, maximum automation, extensive outsourcing) or
div.span: Value Driven ( focused on value creation, premium value proposition)

div.font: Fixed Costs (salaries, rents, utilities),
div.span: Variable costs, Economies of scale, Economies of scope

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(232, 237, 240), rgb(30, 35, 37));

div.font: Revenue Streams
Willing to pay - Users max $4.99. Coaches max $9.99 (provided they get ongoing tips and see new features be implemented)
Channel - Payable via app stores only.

i.font: - Web app - with advertising or partnerships

div.font: For what value are our customers really willing to pay? For what do they currently pay? How are they currently paying? How would they prefer to pay? How much does each Revenue Stream contribute to overall revenues?

Types - Asset sale. Usage fee. Subscription Fees, Lending/Renting/Leasing, Licensing, Brokerage fees, Advertising
Fixed pricing - List Price, Product feature dependent, Customer segment dependent, Volume dependent
Dynamic pricing- Negotiation( bargaining), Yield Management, Real-time-Market
